check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT name FROM (SELECT instance_name AS name FROM v$instance UNION ALL SELECT name FROM v$pdbs) WHERE REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(name), '(8i|9i|10g|11g|12c|18c|19c|21c|23c)');
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle instance names must not contain Oracle version numbers.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT value FROM v$parameter WHERE LOWER(name) = 'remote_os_roles';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: The Oracle REMOTE_OS_ROLES parameter must be set to FALSE.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT value FROM v$parameter WHERE LOWER(name) = 'sql92_security';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: The Oracle SQL92_SECURITY parameter must be set to TRUE.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT CASE WHEN UPPER(value) IN ('EXCLUSIVE','NONE') THEN 'Compliant' END FROM v$parameter WHERE UPPER(name) = 'REMOTE_LOGIN_PASSWORDFILE';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: The Oracle password file ownership and permissions should be limited and the REMOTE_LOGIN_PASSWORDFILE parameter must be set to EXCLUSIVE or NONE.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT privilege FROM dba_sys_privs WHERE grantee = 'PUBLIC';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: System Privileges must not be granted to PUBLIC.

check.command: sh -c 'listeners=$(ps -ef | awk '''/tnslsnr/ && !/awk/ {print $NF}'''); [ -z "$listeners" ] && printf Compliant && exit 0; for l in $listeners; do out=$(lsnrctl status "$l" 2>/dev/null); printf "%s" "$out" | grep -Fq "Security                  ON: Local OS Authentication" || exit 1; printf "%s" "$out" | grep -Fq "Password or Local OS Authentication" && exit 1; printf "%s" "$out" | grep -Fq "Security                  OFF" && exit 1; done; printf Compliant'

checks.description: The Oracle Listener must be configured to require administration authentication.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT granted_role FROM dba_role_privs WHERE grantee = 'PUBLIC';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Application role permissions must not be assigned to the Oracle PUBLIC role.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT value FROM v$parameter WHERE LOWER(name) = '_trace_files_public';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: The Oracle _TRACE_FILES_PUBLIC parameter if present must be set to FALSE.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_unified_enabled_policies WHERE entity_name = 'SYS') >= 1 OR EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM v$parameter WHERE name = 'audit_sys_operations' AND UPPER(value) = 'TRUE') THEN 'Compliant' ELSE 'Finding' END FROM dual;
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Changes to configuration options must be audited.

check.command: sh -c 'f="$ORACLE_HOME/network/admin/cman.ora"; if [ ! -f "$f" ] || grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*REMOTE_ADMIN[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*NO([[:space:]]|$)" "$f"; then printf Compliant; fi'

checks.description: Remote administration must be disabled for the Oracle connection manager.
Network client connections must be restricted to supported versions.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT username FROM SYS.DBA_USERS_WITH_DEFPWD WHERE username <> 'XS$NULL';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle Database default accounts must be assigned custom passwords.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT limit FROM DBA_PROFILES WHERE profile = 'ORA_STIG_PROFILE' AND resource_name = 'PASSWORD_LOCK_TIME';
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle Database must verify account lockouts persist until reset by an administrator.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 0 THEN 'Compliant' ELSE 'Finding' END FROM dba_profiles WHERE resource_name = 'FAILED_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS' AND (UPPER(limit) = 'UNLIMITED' OR TO_NUMBER(limit DEFAULT 999999 ON CONVERSION ERROR) > 3);
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle Database must set the maximum number of consecutive invalid logon attempts to three.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) = 0 THEN 'Compliant' ELSE 'Finding' END FROM (SELECT profile FROM dba_profiles GROUP BY profile HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN UPPER(resource_name) = 'INACTIVE_ACCOUNT_TIME' AND UPPER(limit) <> 'UNLIMITED' AND TO_NUMBER(limit DEFAULT 999999 ON CONVERSION ERROR) <= 35 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0);
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle Database must disable user accounts after 35 days of inactivity.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT DISTINCT username FROM dba_users WHERE username IN ('BI','HR','OE','PM','IX','SH','SCOTT');
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle Database default demonstration and sample databases, database objects, and applications must be removed.

check.command: sh -c 'for f in "${FIPS_HOME:-}"/fips.ora "${ORACLE_HOME:-}/ldap/admin/fips.ora"; do [ -f "$f" ] && grep -Eiq "^[[:space:]]*SSLFIPS_140[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*TRUE([[:space:]]|$)" "$f" && printf Compliant && exit 0; done'

checks.description: Oracle Database must use NIST-validated FIPS 140-2/140-3 compliant cryptography for authentication mechanisms.

check.command:
sqlplus -s / as sysdba <<'SQL'
SET HEADING OFF FEEDBACK OFF PAGESIZE 0 VERIFY OFF ECHO OFF
SELECT log_mode FROM v$database;
EXIT
SQL

checks.description: Oracle Database must preserve any organization-defined system state information in the event of a system failure.
